channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: UK Grid Issues Another Supply Warning
item.description: The UK grid operator issued a second power supply warning for Friday evening.

item.title: Crude Falls On Rising Gulf Supplies
item.description: Oil prices fell sharply as rising Strait of Hormuz traffic and increasing Persian Gulf exports fueled oversupply concerns.

item.title: The Billion-Dollar Debt Deals Exposing an Oil Giant
item.description: Last week, Angola's state oil company, Sociedade Nacional de Combustíveis de Angola (Sonangol), secured a $2.65-billion financing deal with a consortium of international banks to fund the company's operating expenses…

item.title: AI Demand, War, and Climate Pressure Push World Back To Nuclear
item.description: Global energy markets are in turmoil as energy crises keep piling up. The energy-hungry AI boom, war in Iran, geopolitical instability, and climate pressures are creating a polycrisis for the global energy sector, and…

item.title: EIA Sees USA Energy Demand Dropping in 2026, Rising in 2027
item.description: In its latest short term energy outlook, the U.S. Energy Information Administration projected that U.S. total energy consumption will decrease in 2026 then rise in 2027.

item.title: Traffic Flows Through Hormuz Despite Ship Attack
item.description: Traffic continued to flow in both directions through the vital thoroughfare on Friday.

item.title: China's Battery Giant Bets Big on Sodium as Lithium Volatility Persists
item.description: The world’s largest battery maker is taking a step back from the eclectic vehicles sector to refocus its efforts on energy storage. China’s Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. (better known as CATL) has been…

item.title: The IAEA Faces a New Nuclear Puzzle Inside Iran
item.description: Amid an ongoing row between Washington and Tehran over whether international monitors can verify Iranian compliance with its nuclear nonproliferation commitments, former officials have told RFE/RL that the scale, scope…

item.title: US Drillers Add Oil, Gas Rigs As Hormuz Transit Resumes
item.description: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 573, up 26 from this…

item.title: Equinor Pulls Out of Japan’s Offshore Wind Market
item.description: Equinor is ending its offshore wind business activities in Japan and will close its Tokyo office by the end of the year as the Norwegian energy major is realigning its non-oil and gas strategy to focus on power markets…

item.title: Aker Solutions secures five-year engineering deal for White Rose field
item.description: Aker Solutions has entered into a five-year agreement with Cenovus Energy to provide engineering and maintenance services for assets at the White Rose oil field in Canada.

item.title: USA Oil, Gas Workforce Rises from April to May
item.description: The number of employees in the oil and gas extraction industry increased slightly from April to May, data on the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics website showed.

item.title: DOE Plans $17.5B Loans for Westinghouse-Backed Nuclear Projects
item.description: The United States Department of Energy announced a conditional loan commitment of $17.5 billion to help 5 nuclear power projects procure long-lead items to deploy Westinghouse's advanced reactor.

item.title: Canada and Japan Consider Joint Critical Mineral Projects to Counter China
item.description: Canada and Japan are considering cooperation in critical mineral projects and a potential plan of joint stockpiling of key metals as the two G7 economies look to reduce China’s outsized dominance in the market, Canada’s…

item.title: Brent Erases Iran War Premium as Hormuz Flows Show Signs of Recovery
item.description: Oil prices tumbled as markets bet Strait of Hormuz disruptions will ease, with Brent down 10% on the week and Middle East crude benchmarks slipping into contango. Friday, June 26, 2026 Whilst ship transits through the…

item.title: Russia’s Fuel Crisis Is Pushing the Kremlin Toward Kazakhstan
item.description: Grappling with a severe shortage, Russia is seeking to purchase a significant volume of gasoline from neighboring Kazakhstan. Astana appears hesitant to fulfill the request, however, apparently concerned that doing so…

item.title: Var Energi Greenlights Gjoa Projects
item.description: Var Energi and its partners made a final investment decision to proceed with the Gjoa Subsea Projects on Norway's side of the North Sea, targeting about 76 million barrels of oil equivalent gross proven and probable…

item.title: China Isn’t Giving Up On Coal Despite Green Energy Goals
item.description: Chinese authorities are not restricting coal power growth in the new five-year energy plan as Beijing doubles down on the fossil fuel as a “bottom-line guarantee” of the electricity system amid rising shares of…

item.title: Why Is Oil Trading Down Today?
item.description: Why is the oil price going lower on Friday?

item.title: Equinor submits Wisting oilfield EIA for public review
item.description: Equinor and its partners have submitted a proposed environmental impact assessment (EIA) programme for the potential development of the Wisting oil field, which is now open for public consultation.
